# crispyx

> crispyx is a Python package for memory-efficient, streaming analysis of
> large-scale CRISPR and Perturb-seq screens on disk-backed AnnData files.

## Purpose

crispyx solves a specific problem: genome-wide CRISPR screens produce datasets
with hundreds of thousands to millions of cells. Standard tools (Scanpy,
Pertpy) load the full count matrix into RAM, requiring 30–120 GB or more.
crispyx streams directly from .h5ad files on disk so that quality control,
normalization, pseudobulk aggregation, and differential expression all run
without materializing the full matrix.

## Key capabilities

- Streaming QC and preprocessing (filter cells, perturbations, genes;
  normalize and log-transform) without loading the full matrix
- Pseudobulk aggregation: average log expression and pseudobulk count matrices
- Differential expression: t-test, Wilcoxon rank-sum, negative binomial GLM
  with apeGLM log-fold-change shrinkage
- Memory-efficient PCA and KNN graph construction on backed AnnData
- Scanpy-compatible API: cx.pp, cx.pb, cx.tl, cx.pl namespaces
- HPC support: resume/checkpoint, configurable memory_limit_gb, Docker and
  Singularity images

## Intended users

Computational biologists analyzing large-scale CRISPR or Perturb-seq datasets
who cannot load the full count matrix into memory, or who work on HPC systems
with per-job memory limits. Also useful for anyone running multiple DE methods
at scale (t-test, Wilcoxon, NB-GLM) on the same dataset.

## Relationship to other tools

- **vs Scanpy**: crispyx uses the same AnnData-based API and produces
  identical results (Pearson r > 0.9999 for t-test and Wilcoxon), but uses
  2–43x less time and 2–6x less memory by streaming from disk rather than
  loading into RAM.
- **vs Pertpy/PyDESeq2**: crispyx's NB-GLM is 2x faster and runs within 64 GB
  where Pertpy/PyDESeq2 exceeds 120 GB or fails on genome-wide datasets.
  Pearson r > 0.97 vs PyDESeq2 for LFC estimates.
- **vs edgeR**: edgeR times out or errors on most genome-wide datasets tested.
  crispyx completes all 12 benchmark datasets.

## Benchmarks

Benchmarked across 12 public CRISPR screen datasets (21K–1.97M cells):
- crispyx completes all 12 datasets; Scanpy times out or OOMs on large screens;
  Pertpy and edgeR fail on most genome-wide datasets.
- t-test: 2–11x faster than Scanpy with identical results
- Wilcoxon: 2–43x faster than Scanpy with identical results
- NB-GLM: 2x faster than Pertpy, completes where Pertpy runs out of memory

- PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/crispyx

- API reference: https://crispyx.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html
- Benchmarks: https://crispyx.readthedocs.io/en/latest/benchmarking.html
- Comparison: https://crispyx.readthedocs.io/en/latest/comparison.html
